judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with solid grounding; Report 1 additionally notes the images are placeholder text banners, while Report 2 offers a cleaner tabular reconciliation — overall comparable in substance.

report_1.evidence: "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" (line 30) — false; correct or remove.** `app/security/config.py:4` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`... "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" (line 24) — false; remove the section.** `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a `list_visits` endpoint that returns an empty list, plus a `# TODO: pdf export` comment

report_2.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — false.** `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` endpoint returning an empty list and the comment `# TODO: pdf export`... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false.** `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` under the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic."

report_1.evidence: The capture manifest confirms this at the source: `captures.json` records `after_scheduling.png` as captured from route `/billing/invoices`, while `before_scheduling.png` came from `/schedule`.

report_2.evidence: `captures.json` confirms it was captured on route `/billing/invoices` while the "before" was captured on `/schedule`.

report_1.evidence: **`after_export.png` is referenced by the document (line 26) but does not exist.** `captures.json` itself records it as "expected capture; no corresponding file"... **`unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk but is referenced by nothing**

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **No — missing** | Referenced at `highlights-v4.md:26`; `captures.json` itself flags it as "expected capture; no corresponding file"... `img/unused_capture_07.png` exists on disk but is referenced nowhere in the document.

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (line 33).** Two problems: it self-describes the document as a *draft*, which must not appear in a sent customer email regardless of anything else; and it is an attribution/process artifact.

report_2.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance." (`highlights-v4.md:33`)** — an internal draft-provenance note.
